name: AttributeDictionary
summary: The AttributeDictionary class allows you to attach arbitrary collections of attributes to a SketchUp entity. The attributes are defined by key/value pairs where the keys are strings. An Entity or Model object can have any number of AttributeDictionary objects (see the AttributeDictionaries class). The Entity class is a popular parent class in SketchUp, meaning you can attach attribute dictionaries to almost anything, from geometric items like edges and faces and components to more conceptual things like pages or materials.

methods.name: count
methods.summary: The count method is inherited from the Enumerable mix-in module.
methods.remarks: The count method is inherited from the Enumerable mix-in module.

methods.name: delete_key
methods.summary: The delete_key method is used to delete an attribute with a given key.
methods.remarks: The delete_key method is used to delete an attribute with a given key.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: Note: Don't remove content from this collection while iterating over it with #each.
methods.remarks: Note: Don't remove content from this collection while iterating over it with #each. This would change the size of the collection and cause elements to be skipped as the indices change. Instead copy the current collection to an array using to_a and then use each on the array, when removing content. The #each method iterate through all of the attributes.

methods.name: each_key
methods.summary: The #each_key method is used to iterate through all of the attribute keys.
methods.remarks: The #each_key method is used to iterate through all of the attribute keys.

methods.name: each_pair
methods.summary: The #each_pair method is an alias for #each.
methods.remarks: The #each_pair method is an alias for #each.

methods.name: empty?
methods.summary: The #empty? method is used to check if the attribute dictionary is empty.
methods.remarks: The #empty? method is used to check if the attribute dictionary is empty.

returns.doc: true if the attribute dictionary is empty, false otherwise

methods.name: keys
methods.summary: The keys method is used to retrieve an array with all of the attribute keys.
methods.remarks: The keys method is used to retrieve an array with all of the attribute keys.

returns.doc: an array of keys within the attribute dictionary if successful

methods.name: length
methods.summary: The #length method is used to retrieve the size (number of elements) of an attribute dictionary.
methods.remarks: The #length method is used to retrieve the size (number of elements) of an attribute dictionary.

methods.name: name
methods.summary: The name method is used to retrieve the name of an attribute dictionary.
methods.remarks: The name method is used to retrieve the name of an attribute dictionary.

returns.doc: the name of the attribute dictionary if successful

methods.name: size
methods.summary: The #size method is an alias of #length.
methods.remarks: The #size method is an alias of #length.

methods.name: values
methods.summary: The values method is used to retrieve an array with all of the attribute values.
methods.remarks: The values method is used to retrieve an array with all of the attribute values.

returns.doc: an array of values within the attribute dictionary if successful

properties.summary: The [] method is used to retrieve the attribute with a given key.
properties.remarks: The [] method is used to retrieve the attribute with a given key.
